commonMedications.name: Semaglutide / GLP-1 (Ozempic/Wegovy) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D covers for diabetes (tier 3). $25 copay typical. Without coverage ~$900/mo. IRA negotiated price TBD.

commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Olmesartan/Medoxomil (Benicar) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1-2; $4-15 copay

commonMedications.name: Albuterol inhaler [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D tier 2; $10-25 copay. IRA cap applies.

commonMedications.name: Metformin 500mg [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Allopurinol (gout) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Lisinopril [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Topiramate (Topamax) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1-2; $4-15 copay

commonMedications.name: Metoprolol (Lopressor) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Atorvastatin 20mg [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

medicine.pharmacyAccess: Good. CVS, Walgreens, Walmart, Kroger pharmacies available throughout metro Richmond. Costco pharmacy in Short Pump area. Mail-order options through Medicare Part D plans.
medicine.prescriptionSystem: Medicare Part D prescription drug plan. Choose standalone PDP or Medicare Advantage with drug coverage. Annual out-of-pocket cap $2,000 under Inflation Reduction Act.
medicine.insuranceCoverage: Medicare Part D covers most prescriptions with tiered copays. Generic drugs $0-15/month, preferred brands $30-50/month, specialty drugs 25-33% coinsurance up to $2,000 annual cap.

sources.title: Inflation Reduction Act Drug Provisions - KFF

annualExamCost.notes: Original Medicare does NOT cover routine vision exams. Must have separate vision plan or Medicare Advantage with vision.

vision.insuranceCoverage: Need separate vision plan or Medicare Advantage with vision. Standalone vision plans (VSP, EyeMed) run $15-25/month and cover annual exam + $100-200 frame/lens allowance.
vision.specialistWaitTime: 1-3 weeks for routine eye exams. Richmond has good availability of optometrists. VCU Health ophthalmology for specialist care (2-4 week waits).
vision.seniorConsiderations: Medicare covers cataract surgery and glaucoma screening. Annual glaucoma screening covered for high-risk beneficiaries. Diabetic retinopathy screening covered. One pair of eyeglasses or contacts after cataract surgery.

annualCleaningCost.notes: Original Medicare does NOT cover routine dental. Need separate dental plan or Medicare Advantage with dental.

dental.insuranceCoverage: Need separate dental plan ($20-50/month) or Medicare Advantage with dental. Standalone plans (Delta Dental of Virginia, Cigna) typically cover 100% preventive, 50-80% basic, 50% major procedures. Annual maximums $1,000-2,000.
dental.dentistAvailability: Good in Richmond metro. VCU School of Dentistry offers reduced-cost dental care. Wait times typically 1-3 weeks for routine appointments.

sources.title: VCU School of Dentistry - Patient Care

categories.name: Dining Out
categories.seniorDiscounts: AARP restaurant discounts available at many chains.
categories.notes: Lunch for 2 at moderate restaurant ~$32 + 5.3% tax + 20% tip = ~$40/meal, 2x/week = ~$330/mo

categories.name: Cable + 1 Gig Internet
categories.seniorDiscounts: Xfinity and Verizon FiOS available in Richmond.

categories.name: Amazon Prime
categories.seniorDiscounts: Medicaid recipients qualify for Prime at $6.99/mo.

categories.name: Gym/Fitness
categories.seniorDiscounts: SilverSneakers free with many Medicare Advantage plans.

categories.name: Parks/Outdoors
categories.seniorDiscounts: James River Park System free. VA state parks senior discount.

categories.name: Coffee/Cafes
categories.seniorDiscounts: Senior coffee discounts at major chains.

monthlyPass.notes: GRTC Transit monthly pass $64 regular. Seniors 65+ ride for half-fare with Medicare card. Pulse BRT (Broad Street) runs every 10-15 minutes.

publicTransit.coverage: GRTC bus network covers most of Richmond metro. Pulse BRT rapid transit along Broad Street corridor. Coverage limited in suburban Henrico and Chesterfield counties. Expanding with new routes.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: Seniors 65+ get half-fare on all GRTC routes with Medicare card or GRTC senior ID. Half-fare on Pulse BRT as well.

rideShare.availability: Good in Richmond metro area. Uber and Lyft available. May have longer waits in suburban areas.
rideShare.notes: GoGoGrandparent available for seniors. Some wait times longer than major metros, especially late night.

registration.notes: Virginia charges personal property tax on vehicles, assessed by locality. City of Richmond rate $3.70 per $100 of assessed value. On a $25,000 car this is ~$925/year. Henrico County $3.50/$100. State registration $33-53/year.

insurance.notes: Virginia car insurance slightly above national average but lower than NoVA. Richmond rates moderate. Senior safe driver discounts available (5-15%).

fuel.notes: ~$3.40/gallon average in Richmond. VA gas tax $0.294/gallon. Typical senior drives 8,000-12,000 miles/year.

parking.notes: Free parking in most suburban areas. Downtown Richmond metered parking $1.50/hour. Monthly garage parking downtown $80-150.

tolls.notes: Minimal tolls in Richmond area. Pocahontas Parkway (Route 895) $3.60 toll. Downtown Expressway $0.75. Much less than NoVA toll burden.

carOwnership.seniorDiscounts: Virginia Safe Driver discount for completing AARP Smart Driver course (5-15% insurance reduction). City of Richmond offers vehicle property tax relief for qualifying low-income seniors.

transportation.walkability: Moderate. Fan District, Carytown, and downtown areas are walkable. Suburban areas (Short Pump, Midlothian) are car-dependent. Pulse BRT improving transit-oriented development.

cycling.infrastructure: Growing bike lane network. Capital Trail (52-mile paved trail to Williamsburg) excellent for recreation. Virginia Capital Trail is a major asset. City adding protected bike lanes.
cycling.notes: Richmond Bike Share discontinued but trails are excellent. Capital Trail is one of the best urban trails on the East Coast.

sources.title: City of Richmond Personal Property Tax

electricVehicle.purchaseNotes: Tesla Model 3 ~$35K, Model Y ~$45K, Hyundai Ioniq 5 ~$42K, Chevy Equinox EV ~$28K. Federal tax credit up to $7,500 (income limits apply). Some states add incentives.
electricVehicle.incentiveNotes: Federal EV tax credit up to $7,500 under IRA (income < $150K single / $300K joint). Must be new vehicle, MSRP < $55K sedan / $80K SUV. Used EV credit up to $4,000.

chargingHome.notes: Level 2 (240V) home charger. Electricity ~$0.12-0.16/kWh. ~1,000 mi/month = ~300 kWh = $36-48/mo.

chargingPublic.notes: Tesla Supercharger ~$0.40-0.50/kWh. Electrify America ~$0.43/kWh. ChargePoint varies. Free L2 chargers at some retailers.

insurance.notes: EV insurance 15-25% higher than comparable ICE vehicle. Shop Liberty Mutual, Progressive, Tesla Insurance where available.

maintenance.notes: No oil changes, fewer brake jobs (regen braking). Tire rotation every 7,500 mi. Cabin air filter annually. Brake fluid every 2-3 yrs. 12V battery every 4-5 yrs (~$100). Tires wear faster on heavier EVs.

registration.notes: Many states add EV-specific fees ($100-275/yr) to replace lost gas tax revenue. Virginia: $64/yr EV fee.

electricVehicle.batteryWarranty: 8 years / 100,000 miles (federal minimum). Most manufacturers cover 70-80% capacity retention.
electricVehicle.depreciationNotes: EVs depreciate 40-50% in first 3 years, though Tesla holds value better. Used EV market improving. Budget ~$3,000-5,000/yr depreciation on $35K vehicle.
electricVehicle.totalMonthlyNotes: Includes: charging ($50), insurance ($180), maintenance ($50/mo avg), registration ($17/mo). Excludes: purchase/loan payment, depreciation.

discounts.name: Medicare
discounts.description: Federal health insurance at 65. Part A (hospital) premium-free for most. Part B (medical) ~$185/month. Part D (drugs) $10-50/month.

discounts.name: AARP Membership
discounts.description: Annual membership $16. Discounts on insurance, dining, travel, retail, and entertainment. Available at age 50+.

discounts.name: Virginia Social Security Tax Exemption
discounts.description: Virginia fully exempts Social Security benefits from state income tax. Additional $12,000 age deduction per person at 65+.

discounts.name: GRTC Senior Transit Fare
discounts.description: Half-price bus and Pulse BRT fares for riders 65+. Present Medicare card or GRTC senior ID.

discounts.name: Richmond Senior Services
discounts.description: City of Richmond Senior Centers offer free programs, meals, health screenings, and social activities. Senior Connections (Area Agency on Aging) coordinates services.

discounts.name: Virginia Personal Property Tax Relief
discounts.description: Richmond offers property tax relief and deferral programs for seniors meeting income requirements. City provides partial exemption for vehicles.

discounts.name: America the Beautiful Senior Pass
discounts.description: Lifetime pass to all national parks and federal recreation areas for $80 (or $20/year). Richmond is near multiple national parks (Shenandoah, Colonial NHP).

discounts.name: SilverSneakers Fitness
discounts.description: Free gym membership through participating Medicare Advantage and Medigap plans. Access to 17,000+ locations nationwide.

plans.name: T-Mobile 55+ Essentials
plans.notes: 2 lines unlimited talk/text/data. VA wireless tax ~9% + federal USF. 5G included.

breakdown.notes: Water, sewage, and trash are separate utility bills — see utilities breakdown.

housing.taxNotes: Virginia has no rental tax. Richmond city property tax rate ~$1.20/$100 assessed value — landlord responsibility. No additional renter taxes or fees.
housing.leaseTerms: 12-month standard lease. Security deposit max 2 months rent ($3,600). Virginia Residential Landlord and Tenant Act applies.
housing.marketNotes: Affordable Southern city with good 2BR house availability. Fan District, Museum District, and Near West End popular for walkability. Prices rising but still well below DC metro.

sources.name: City of Richmond Real Estate Tax

stores.name: South of the James Farmers Market

videos.title: South of the James Market RVA

items.name: Chicken (whole/thighs)
items.notes: Primary protein for both adults & dog

items.name: Ground Turkey / Beef
items.notes: Lean ground for dog food base

items.name: Organ Meat (liver/heart)
items.notes: Essential for balanced home-cooked dog diet

healthcare.coverageSummary: Medicare Part A (free) + Part B ($174.70/mo pp) + Medigap Plan G (~$200/mo pp) + Part D (~$45/mo pp). IRA out-of-pocket cap $2,000/yr starting 2025.

costs.notes: Higher due to AMD + multiple conditions. Age-rated.
Medicare covers 80%, Medigap covers 20% coinsurance. ~6-8 injections/yr at $40-50 copay each.
OCT scans every 4-8 weeks. Medicare + Medigap covers most.
Quarterly bloodwork/visits. Low copay with Medigap.
Annual visit + as-needed. Mild asthma, infrequent.
Slightly lower — no AMD, but diabetes management
All generics — lower tier plan sufficient
Quarterly A1C, bloodwork. Covered well by Medicare.
Medicare Part B covers glucose monitors. Some copays.

shared.notes: Separate dental plan ~$35/person. Medicare does not cover dental.
Basic vision plan. AMD specialist visits covered by Medicare Part B.
Covered 100% by Medicare — no copay for preventive.

blackHairCare.label: Black Hair Care
blackHairCare.notes: Richmond has a vibrant Black community with excellent salon options, particularly in the Jackson Ward and Church Hill neighborhoods.

providers.name: Natural Xpressions Hair Studio
providers.notes: Natural hair specialist. Locs, twists, color, silk press. Black-owned.

providers.name: Lumiere Hair Lounge
providers.notes: All textures welcome. Color specialist on staff. Relaxers, weaves, braids.

whiteHairCare.label: White/Caucasian Hair Care
whiteHairCare.notes: Short Pump and Carytown areas have the best salon concentration.

providers.name: Mango Salon
providers.notes: Color bar specialists. Balayage, highlights, grey blending. Eco-friendly products.

providers.name: Floyd's 99 Barbershop
providers.notes: Good for cuts and basic color. Walk-in friendly. Moderate prices.

mensBigTall.label: Men's Big & Tall Clothing
mensBigTall.notes: DXL in Short Pump area. Belk and JCPenney at Chesterfield Towne Center carry some extended sizes.

providers.name: DXL Big + Tall
providers.notes: Full range of big & tall. Casual to dress. Sizes to 8XL.

providers.name: Belk (Chesterfield Towne Center)
providers.notes: Extended sizes section. Good for dress shirts and southern casual.

womensClothing.label: Women's Regular Clothing
womensClothing.notes: Short Pump Town Center and Carytown boutiques offer the best selection.

providers.name: Nordstrom (Short Pump Town Center)
providers.notes: Full women's regular size range. Good alterations. Personal shoppers available.

providers.name: Carytown Boutiques
providers.notes: Multiple indie boutiques. Unique clothing, local designers. Regular sizes.

shoes.label: Shoes
shoes.notes: Short Pump has the best shoe shopping concentration.

providers.name: DSW (Short Pump)
providers.notes: Full selection including wide widths. Men's extended sizes.

providers.name: Nordstrom Rack
providers.notes: Discounted name-brand shoes. Good for dress and casual in all sizes.
